name: trade_settlement
description: a T+1 trade-settlement lifecycle world; actions match, affirm, settle, fail

card.description: a T+1 trade-settlement lifecycle world; actions match, affirm, settle, fail

rules: A trade is born 'executed' on trade date T (current_day == trade_date == 0).
'match' moves an executed trade to 'matched' by reconciling counterparty economic details; it is a no-op from any other status.
'affirm' moves a matched trade to 'affirmed' and rolls the cycle forward to the settlement date (current_day := settlement_date), i.e. T+1.

'settle' is delivery-versus-payment: it is permitted only when the trade is affirmed and current_day >= settlement_date, and only if the seller holds enough securities and the buyer holds enough cash. On success the securities and cash legs move atomically and the trade becomes 'settled'.
If 'settle' is attempted but a leg cannot be delivered, the trade becomes 'failed' with fail_reason 'insufficient_securities' or 'insufficient_cash'.
'fail' explicitly marks a live (matched or affirmed) trade as 'failed' to settle, e.g. a short position or unmatched instruction.
'settled' and 'failed' are terminal: no action changes a trade once it reaches either.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name")
    params = action.get("params") or {}

    status = s.get("status", "executed")
    day = s.get("current_day", 0)
    sett_date = s.get("settlement_date", 1)

    # 'settled' and 'failed' are terminal -- the trade is off the books.
    if status in ("settled", "failed"):
        return s

    if name == "match":
        # Counterparties compare economic details on trade date. Only an
        # executed (unmatched) trade can be matched.
        if status == "executed":
            s["status"] = "matched"
            s["matched"] = True
        return s

    if name == "affirm":
        # Both sides affirm the matched economics, then the cycle rolls
        # overnight to the settlement date (T+1).
        if status == "matched":
            s["status"] = "affirmed"
            s["affirmed"] = True
            s["current_day"] = sett_date
        return s

    if name == "settle":
        # DvP settlement: only once affirmed, only on/after the settlement
        # date, and only if BOTH legs can be delivered. The exchange is atomic.
        if status == "affirmed" and day >= sett_date:
            qty = s.get("quantity", 0)
            cash = s.get("cash_amount", 0.0)
            enough_securities = s.get("seller_inventory", 0) >= qty
            enough_cash = s.get("buyer_cash", 0.0) >= cash
            if enough_securities and enough_cash:
                s["seller_inventory"] = s.get("seller_inventory", 0) - qty
                s["buyer_securities"] = s.get("buyer_securities", 0) + qty
                s["buyer_cash"] = round(s.get("buyer_cash", 0.0) - cash, 2)
                s["seller_cash"] = round(s.get("seller_cash", 0.0) + cash, 2)
                s["status"] = "settled"
                s["settled"] = True
            else:
                # A leg cannot be delivered -> the settlement fails.
                s["status"] = "failed"
                s["failed"] = True
                s["fail_reason"] = ("insufficient_securities"
                                    if not enough_securities
                                    else "insufficient_cash")
        return s

    if name == "fail":
        # An explicit settlement fail (short position, DK, late instruction)
        # from any live, non-settled state.
        if status in ("matched", "affirmed"):
            s["status"] = "failed"
            s["failed"] = True
            if not s.get("fail_reason"):
                s["fail_reason"] = params.get("reason", "settlement_fail")
        return s

    return s

emit.report: Trade status: {status} (day {current_day} of T+{settlement_date}). {quantity} units @ cash {cash_amount}. {fail_reason}
